articles.title: Rockets Coach Defends Controversial Deandre Ayton Ejection
articles.original_title: Rockets Coach Takes Shocking Stance on Lakers’ Deandre Ayton Ejection - heavy.com
articles.summary: Houston Rockets head coach publicly defended the officials’ decision to eject Suns center Deandre Ayton during a recent matchup with the Los Angeles Lakers, calling the call “necessary.” The coach’s remarks sparked debate among fans and analysts about the consistency of officiating in the league.
key_points: Ayton was ejected for a flagrant foul after a hard foul on a Lakers player.
Rockets coach argued the ejection was justified despite criticism.
The incident reignited discussions on officiating standards in the NBA.

articles.title: Lakers Fall to Rockets 115-96 on April 26, 2026
articles.original_title: Los Angeles Lakers vs. Houston Rockets Live Score and Stats - April 26, 2026 Gametracker - CBS Sports
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers were defeated by the Houston Rockets 115-96 in a regular‑season game on April 26, 2026. The game log shows the Rockets outscored the Lakers in every quarter, with the Lakers struggling offensively throughout.
key_points: Final score: Rockets 115, Lakers 96.
Lakers shot under 40% from the field and committed 18 turnovers.
Houston’s bench contributed 30 points, highlighting depth advantage.

articles.title: Lakers Receive ‘Great News’ on Luka Dončić After Game‑4 Loss
articles.original_title: Lakers Get ‘Great News’ on Luka Doncic After Game 4 Loss to Rockets - heavy.com
articles.summary: Following a Game 4 loss to the Rockets, the Lakers received an update indicating that Dallas Mavericks star Luka Dončić is healthy and expected to play in the upcoming games. The news was welcomed by Lakers staff as it could affect future matchups and league dynamics.
key_points: Luka Dončić cleared to play after missing time with a minor injury.
The update was announced after the Lakers’ loss to Houston.
Lakers management noted the news could impact upcoming playoff scenarios.

articles.title: Even Rockets Call Ayton’s Ejection ‘Soft,’ LA Times Reports
articles.original_title: Even the Rockets thought Deandre Ayton’s controversial ejection was ‘soft’ - Los Angeles Times
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Times reported that even members of the Houston Rockets organization described Deandre Ayton’s ejection as a “soft” call, suggesting the decision may have been harsher than warranted. The commentary adds another perspective to the ongoing controversy surrounding the incident.
key_points: Rockets insiders felt the ejection was overly severe.
Ayton’s ejection remains a point of contention among teams and media.
The league has not issued an official statement clarifying the call.

articles.title: Game Recap: Rockets 115, Lakers 96 (April 26, 2026)
articles.original_title: Rockets 115-96 Lakers (Apr 26, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN
articles.summary: ESPN’s recap of the April 26 game highlights the Rockets’ dominant performance, outpacing the Lakers in scoring, rebounding, and defensive efficiency. The Lakers struggled to generate offense, leading to a 19‑point defeat.
key_points: Rockets led by 20 points at halftime and never looked back.
LeBron James scored 22 points but shot 8‑of‑22.
Houston’s Jalen Green led all scorers with 28 points.
